sources.html: Society for American Soccer History — Ed Farnsworth, “Len Oliver Q&A: Playing in the pros in 1950s Philadelphia and beyond” (June 25, 2015) — primary-source interview confirming the Philadelphia Nationals folded before Oliver could play pro for them, his signing with Jimmy Mills at the Uhrik Truckers for “$35 a game in a brown envelope,” and the archive’s description of the 1954-55 Uhrik Truckers championship side as “a group of relative unknowns, many of whom were products of the legendary Lighthouse Boys Club”: ussoccerhistory.org/len-oliver-qa-playing-in-the-pros-in-1950s-philadelphia-and-beyond
sources.text: Society for American Soccer History — Ed Farnsworth, “Len Oliver Q&A: Playing in the pros in 1950s Philadelphia and beyond” (June 25, 2015) — primary-source interview confirming the Philadelphia Nationals folded before Oliver could play pro for them, his signing with Jimmy Mills at the Uhrik Truckers for “$35 a game in a brown envelope,” and the archive’s description of the 1954-55 Uhrik Truckers championship side as “a group of relative unknowns, many of whom were products of the legendary Lighthouse Boys Club”: ussoccerhistory.org/len-oliver-qa-playing-in-the-pros-in-1950s-philadelphia-and-beyond
